input.topic: Stripe deposit received — 14-day build clock starts now. Intake form link + preview-URL ETA in the body.
input.scenario: Transactional confirmation triggered by Stripe checkout.session.completed (metadata event=deposit). Customer just paid. Goal: confirm receipt, kick off the 14-day clock, set 18-hour expectations.
input.voice: Operator. No 'thank you for choosing us' — Jack already has the money. Plain, concrete, signed '— Jack / Day14'.

current_subject: Got your deposit. Build clock starts now.

variants.subject: Deposit in. 14-day clock starts today.
variants.rationale: Two short clauses, both pure receipt. Names the day-count explicitly so the customer's expectation is anchored before they open the body.

variants.subject: Got it — building starts now
variants.rationale: Shortest of the three. Mobile-preview safe. Reads like a text message, not an email — fits the Day14 operator voice.

variants.subject: Deposit received — two things from me in 18 hours
variants.rationale: Curiosity hook on 'two things' previews the numbered list in the body. The 18-hour figure earns the open by promising specific next steps.
